cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 18th-century European intellectual movement emphasizing reason, individual rights, and challenging traditional authority

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The political philosophy that government should protect natural rights (life, liberty, property) and derive authority from the consent of the governed

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: John Locke
cards.answer: English Enlightenment philosopher who argued that people have natural rights to life, liberty, and property, and that government exists to protect these rights
alternate_answers: Philosopher of natural rights and social contract

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The colonial uprising against British rule (1775–1783) that established the United States as an independent republic

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American Declaration of Independence signed

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1789 revolution against the French monarchy that established a republic, ended feudalism, and spread Enlightenment ideals across Europe

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Storming of the Bastille — beginning of the French Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The radical phase of the French Revolution (1793–1794) in which the government executed thousands of perceived enemies of the revolution, including King Louis XVI

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Napoleon Bonaparte
cards.answer: French military leader who rose from the Revolution, became Emperor, spread French legal codes and Enlightenment ideas across Europe through conquest (1799–1815)
alternate_answers: French emperor who spread revolutionary ideals

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The slave rebellion in Saint-Domingue (1791–1804) that created Haiti, the first Black republic and only successful slave revolution in history

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Toussaint Louverture
cards.answer: Formerly enslaved leader who led the Haitian Revolution and organized the rebellion against French colonial rule, inspiring independence movements throughout the Americas

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Haitian independence declared

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The wave of independence movements across Latin America (1810–1830s) inspired by Enlightenment ideas and the American and French Revolutions

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Simon Bolivar
cards.answer: South American revolutionary leader who liberated Venezuela, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Bolivia from Spanish rule and envisioned a unified Latin American federation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Jose de San Martin
cards.answer: South American revolutionary who helped liberate Argentina, Chile, and Peru from Spanish colonial rule

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: American-born Spaniards who led most Latin American independence movements, resentful of colonial restrictions on their political and economic power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The ideological movement emphasizing shared culture, language, and history as the basis for political independence and self-determination

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The transformation from agrarian to industrial production using machines, factories, and fossil fuels, beginning in Britain in the late 18th century

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Industrial Revolution begins in Britain

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: James Watt's invention that powered factories, mines, and locomotives, enabling the Industrial Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The economic system in which private individuals own the means of production and compete for profit in free markets

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The economic ideology critiquing capitalism and calling for collective ownership of the means of production, developed by Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Karl Marx
cards.answer: German philosopher who with Engels wrote The Communist Manifesto (1848) and Das Kapital, arguing capitalism would be overthrown by the working class

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1848 political revolutions that swept Europe, driven by nationalism, liberalism, and working-class grievances, mostly failing but demonstrating popular political power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The abolition of slavery and serfdom in the 19th century, driven by Enlightenment ideas, religious movements, and economic changes

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Emancipation of Russian serfs by Tsar Alexander II

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Qing Dynasty government's attempt to modernize China in the 1860s–1890s by adopting Western technology while preserving Chinese culture

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1868 Japanese modernization revolution that abolished the shogunate and restored imperial power, rapidly industrializing Japan by adopting Western models

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Meiji Restoration in Japan

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1857 uprising by Indian soldiers against British East India Company rule, leading to direct British Crown control over India

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The American Revolution — Declaration of Independence

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The French Revolution begins

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Haitian Revolution — first successful slave revolution in world history

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Napoleon Bonaparte
cards.answer: French military and political leader who spread revolutionary ideas (nationalism, legal equality) across Europe through conquest, but also restored aspects of traditional authority

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The idea that people sharing a common language, culture, and history constitute a natural political community that deserves self-government

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The first Industrial Revolution innovation that used steam power to mechanize production, transforming first textiles then other industries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The early Industrial Revolution shift of production from homes and small workshops to large factories, creating the modern working class

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The political philosophy emphasizing individual rights, representative government, and protection of private property, associated with thinkers like Locke and Voltaire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels' economic and political theory predicting the overthrow of capitalism by the working class and establishment of a classless society

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Publication of The Communist Manifesto by Marx and Engels

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ottoman state reforms (1839–1876) that attempted to modernize the empire by creating a constitutional government, professional army, and legal equality for all subjects

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Japanese government policy (1868 onwards) of rapid industrialization and Westernization to resist European imperialism

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Japan's Meiji Restoration begins the modernization of Japan

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A Chinese reformist movement (1861–1895) that attempted to modernize Chinese military and industry while preserving Confucian social values

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1857 rebellion of Indian soldiers (sepoys) against British East India Company rule that resulted in the transfer of India to direct British Crown administration

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The series of Latin American independence movements (1810–1830) that freed most of Spanish America from colonial rule

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Simón Bolívar
cards.answer: Venezuelan military and political leader who liberated much of South America from Spanish rule, known as the Liberator; envisioned a unified Latin American republic

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Toussaint Louverture
cards.answer: Leader of the Haitian Revolution (1791–1803), the only successful slave revolt in history; Haitian independence in 1804 created the first Black republic in the Western Hemisphere

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The economic and social changes accompanying industrialization: urbanization, rise of working class, new gender roles, environmental degradation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Congress of Vienna redraws Europe's political map after the Napoleonic Wars
